name: Control One bank baseline NetBird policy intent
description: Production policy templates for a bank-owned self-hosted NetBird deployment connected to Control One imports.
guardrails: Disable or remove the default all-to-all policy before go-live.
Use explicit source and destination groups for every allow policy.
Assign every routed subnet or network resource to an access-control group.
Separate routing peers from human admin groups.
Require ticket references and expiry for break-glass membership.

groups.name: secops-admins
groups.purpose: Security administrators allowed to manage hardened hosts.

groups.name: platform-admins
groups.purpose: Platform administrators allowed to reach management ports.

groups.name: app-team
groups.purpose: Application operators allowed to reach app subnets and app ports.

groups.name: db-admins
groups.purpose: Database administrators allowed to reach database listeners.

groups.name: break-glass
groups.purpose: Time-boxed emergency responders with ticket-backed membership.

groups.name: route-prod
groups.purpose: Headless routing peers that bridge production private networks.

groups.name: prod-mgmt-acl
groups.purpose: Route access-control group for production management CIDRs.

groups.name: prod-app-acl
groups.purpose: Route access-control group for production application CIDRs.

groups.name: prod-db-acl
groups.purpose: Route access-control group for production database CIDRs.

templates.name: Disable default all-to-all
templates.intent: No enabled policy may allow All to All across all protocols and ports.
evidence: No enabled policy has source group All and destination group All with protocol any.
Control One import shows zero unrestricted full-mesh policies.

templates.name: Production management access
guards: MFA required by IdP or local-user policy.
Source group membership reviewed at least monthly.
Route must use an access-control group; unrestricted route distribution is not permitted.

templates.name: Application team to production app subnets
guards: No SSH or database ports in this template.
Application subnet CIDRs must be approved in the bank CMDB.
Control One must reconcile reachable services with expected app inventory.

templates.name: Database administration access
guards: Database admin membership requires manager and security approval.
No broad app-team access to database groups.
Database routes must not be distributed to the All group.

templates.name: Routing peer hardening
guards: Routing peers use setup keys, not personal logins.
Routing peers are patched and monitored as production infrastructure.
Routing peer host firewalls allow forwarding only for approved routed CIDRs.
Routing peers are not members of human admin groups.

templates.name: Emergency break-glass access
guards: Ticket id is recorded in group membership notes.
Control One alert is generated when the group is non-empty.
